prerequisites: A list of one or more public web page URLs to analyze (the connector's required `urls` field).
One or more PageSpeed Insights categories to request via the required `categories` field. The API accepts ACCESSIBILITY, BEST_PRACTICES, PERFORMANCE, and SEO.
One or more analysis strategies via the required `strategies` field. The API accepts DESKTOP and MOBILE.
Optional, recommended for frequent or automated queries: a Google API key. The API can be used with or without a key.
Optional, to obtain a key: a Google Cloud project with access to the Credentials page (console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials).

authMethods.name: API key (optional)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the connector's optional `api_key` secret field. The PageSpeed Insights API can be called with or without a key, but Google recommends a key for frequent, automated queries. When set, the key is appended to request URLs as the `key=yourAPIKey` query parameter. Google states the key is safe to embed in URLs and needs no encoding. Leave the field empty to call the API without a key.

setupSteps.title: Decide whether you need an API key
setupSteps.body: The PageSpeed Insights API works with or without an API key. For occasional, manual use you can leave the connector's `api_key` field empty. Google recommends supplying a key for frequent or automated extraction.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key (if using one)
setupSteps.body: Open the Google Cloud Credentials page at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials. Create a new API key in a Google Cloud project. Copy the key value and paste it into the connector's `api_key` field.

setupSteps.title: Set the target URLs
setupSteps.body: Populate the required `urls` field with the public web page URLs you want analyzed. Each URL is sent to the runPagespeed endpoint as the required `url` parameter.

setupSteps.title: Choose categories
setupSteps.body: Populate the required `categories` field. Allowed values are ACCESSIBILITY, BEST_PRACTICES, PERFORMANCE, and SEO. These map to the API's `category` query parameter, which can be repeated to request multiple Lighthouse categories.

setupSteps.title: Choose strategies
setupSteps.body: Populate the required `strategies` field. Allowed values are DESKTOP and MOBILE, mapping to the API's `strategy` query parameter that selects the analysis profile.

setupSteps.title: Mind the API quota
setupSteps.body: The API enforces a usage quota, and Google recommends a key for frequent, automated queries to obtain higher limits. Review the official quota documentation before running large or scheduled extractions.

sources.title: Get Started with the PageSpeed Insights API
PageSpeed Insights API: runPagespeed REST reference (category and strategy enums)
